note: S0 batvision-reference grid opened: 2ch/5ch x log/nolog under the new PLANAR target. Anchors the reference number RayDPT must beat.
Depth target switched RADIAL -> PLANAR (cubemap perpendicular-Z); radial_factor removed from prepare._depth (87b3047). Operator instruction. No metric from before 87b3047 is comparable.
geometric-head refuted BEFORE running: measured grad-energy shows planar (0.01618) is smoother than radial (0.01677); radial smoother in only 17% of samples. Premise false. Zero GPU time spent.
batvision 2ch nolog: composite 1.8854 (rmse 1.3186, d1 0.5785, abs_rel 0.4143), best epoch 14/26.
Best at epoch 14/26 then 12 epochs of overfitting; val loss rises 0.1871->0.1925. Cosine sized for 40 epochs, only 26 fit the budget. Schedule NOT changed mid-grid -- all four cells must share it.
batvision 2ch log: composite 1.8784, delta vs E0 = 0.0070 < sigma 0.008. Metrics split across the two runs. Crown neither.
log1p compression is a no-op at 2ch, contradicting the pre-registered prediction that it would help MORE at 2ch than 5ch. Await E2/E3 before interpreting.

Two grid cells concluded and both produced unexplained results (D1: a pre-registered prediction failed; D2: a budget confound affecting every future run). Before spending the remaining GPU on more of the same lineage, step up a level and ask what the evidence actually supports.

Abstraction levels reviewed: input representation / target geometry / decoder resolution / optimisation schedule / sensing physics. The physics level was never examined. MEASURED: the STFT gives only T=18 time frames over the 58.8ms window, nearest-upsampled 28.4x to the 512-wide axis; hop=160 quantises one-way depth at 0.567m while the best RMSE is 1.3116m. Time-of-flight resolution is a candidate binding constraint that no amount of decoder work can relieve.

Temporal resolution of the input (hop 160 -> 40). Causally FAR from the current decoder/attention lineage, grounded in sensing physics, nearly free in model cost, and falsifiable on the cheapest parent (run_base.py).

Serial scored-evaluation lock added (utils/evallock.py). Our composite has no runtime term, but TIME_BUDGET is wall-clock, so overlapping runs fit fewer epochs and stop being comparable. Infrastructure -- NOT recorded as a research result.

Upgrade-plan section 15 (audio representation search-space): STFT analysis window moved from prepare.py module constants into cfg.dataset (--stft-nfft/--stft-hop/--stft-win). Fixed responsibilities untouched: _wave's cut depends only on (c, window_m, sr). Defaults verified BIT-IDENTICAL to the historical representation. Unblocks I1. Infrastructure -- not a research result.

S2 (queued, explore): I1 temporal-resolution probe on the CHEAPEST parent (batvision, not the champion). Control is E2 (batvision_5ch_nolog, hop=160). Probes: hop=80 and hop=40. PRE-REGISTERED falsification: RMSE must fall monotonically 160->80->40. If ABS_REL improves but RMSE does not, the gain is not time-of-flight and I1 is DROPped.

S1 (queued, exploit): E4 re-anchors RayDPT (my model) under the planar target. Prerequisite for every RayDPT improvement; nothing before 87b3047 is comparable. Runs first so I2 (decoder resolution) has a valid parent.

batvision 5ch nolog: composite 1.8646 (rmse 1.3207, d1 0.5938, abs_rel 0.4460), best epoch 13/25. Beats both 2ch cells (1.8854, 1.8784) by 0.014-0.021, ABOVE sigma~0.008: the derived interaural cues (ILD, cosIPD, sinIPD) DO add information.

I2 (decoder output resolution) REFUTED by an oracle diagnostic costing zero GPU. A PERFECT predictor at RayDPT's 64x128 decode resolution, bilinearly upsampled, still scores d1=0.9757 / RMSE=0.2873m / composite=0.2399. Achieved is d1=0.5938 / RMSE=1.3207 / composite=1.8646. The model is nowhere near the upsample ceiling, so the 4x bilinear upsample is NOT the binding constraint. Going to 128x256 would move a ceiling the model cannot reach.

D3: E2's composite win over the 2ch cells is entirely a d1 win (0.5938 vs 0.5808); its RMSE (1.3207) is WORSE than E1's (1.3116) and its ABS_REL is the worst of the three. Derived interaural cues improve angular assignment (ILD/IPD encode azimuth), not range. Range accuracy remains unexplained -- which is what I1 targets.

I1 probe REDESIGNED before spending GPU. The STFT's temporal resolution is set by the analysis WINDOW, not the hop: win=400 smears an echo over 1.417m of one-way depth (~= the achieved RMSE 1.3207m), and shrinking the hop alone leaves that smear untouched, merely oversampling. New two-arm design dissociates density from resolution: A (win400,hop40) = density only; B (win64,hop16) = 0.227m smear. Only B should improve RMSE if I1 is right.

Failed instrument: a training-free NN-retrieval probe intended to test I1 cheaply is INVALID -- its retrieval (rmse 2.20) is worse than the trivial mean-training-depth predictor (rmse 1.64), so it has no power to detect anything. Recorded so it is not mistaken for evidence against I1.
batvision 5ch log: composite 1.8567 (rmse 1.3088, d1 0.5949, abs_rel 0.4517), best ep13/25. Best of the 2x2; reference champion.

S0 PASS. 2x2 dissociation: interaural cues buy d1 (angle, +0.015), log1p buys rmse (range, -0.010); effects ADDITIVE. Predicted log-x-channel-count interaction REFUTED -- ILD's log ratio does not make compression redundant. Reference anchored at 1.8567 (E3).

RayDPT's DPT skips add encoder features to the ray grid by pixel index, equating (frequency,time) with (elevation,azimuth) -- a false spatial correspondence. The global cross-attention does not have this problem. Diagnostic (zero GPU) first: azimuthal FFT of predictions for a peak at the 18-block staircase period.

I5's pre-registered signature is ABSENT: azimuthal FFT of E3 predictions shows no peak at the 18-block staircase frequency k=18 (prominence 0.806, a dip) nor at harmonics. Measured on batvision, which also mixes encoder features by pixel index, so this is real evidence against 'pixel-index skips inject the staircase'. Parked as inconclusive; no GPU spent.

D4 resolved: the model is a LOW-PASS predictor (97.4% of azimuthal power in k<=6 vs GT 75.8%; ~5% of GT power at k>=17). The apparent banding is the k=4 four-wall structure, over-represented 1.94x. Two competing explanations registered: I6 (the objective's low-frequency auxiliaries carry 58.2% of the gradient) and I7 (two microphones under-determine fine azimuth). I6's ablation discriminates BOTH -- one run decides two hypotheses.
Objective is 58.2% low-frequency at convergence (coarse-layout 38.4% + low-pass 19.7% vs dense 41.8%). Exposed --w-coarse-layout / --w-low as CLI flags (defaults unchanged) and queued the ablation.

Competing explanation for the same low-pass observation: two microphones give a broad directional response, so fine azimuthal structure may be near-unobservable. NOT a task ceiling -- a scoped, falsifiable claim about angular observability, decided by I6's ablation.
RayDPT planar anchor: composite 2.0471 (rmse 1.3987, d1 0.5423), but ONLY 5 epochs fit (713.5s/ep vs batvision 130s). Best = last epoch, still improving. Not a verdict on the architecture.

D5: RayDPT is compute-starved. Under a wall-clock budget throughput is silently part of the score. 64x128 attention (lsa64+cross64) = 57% of forward; F.unfold materialises 1.8GB. Also 68.7% of its params are dead (e5..e8, no gradient).

I1 arm A (win400 hop40, density only, smear UNCHANGED at 1.417m): composite 1.8514 vs control E2 1.8646, delta +0.0132 ABOVE sigma. RMSE improved -0.0227, d1 flat -0.0019. The arm that was supposed to show NO effect improved RMSE. Per pre-registration this weakens the resolution story: density alone moves the range metric. Await arm B.

D6: I1's CONTROL arm improved. E5 (win400 hop40) cut RMSE 0.0227 and composite 0.0132 (above sigma) with the temporal smear UNCHANGED at 1.417m. Nyquist says hop160 already samples the envelope at 1.2x Nyquist, so arm A added ~no information. Third explanation registered as I10: the nearest-neighbour resize in _features turns the time axis into a 28.4px staircase; bilinear at hop=160 holds information fixed and removes only the staircase.

I1 arm B (win64 hop16, 6.2x finer smear): composite 1.8379, best batvision so far. But the gain is d1 (+0.0067) and abs_rel, NOT rmse -- its rmse (1.3114) is WORSE than arm A's (1.2980), which changed no resolution at all. I1's pre-registered metric prediction is REFUTED: temporal resolution buys angle, not range.

D7: the two I1 arms improved through OPPOSITE metrics, inverting the physical story. The only variable monotone with the composite across E2/E5/E6 is staircase block width (28.4->7.2->2.9 px giving 1.8646->1.8514->1.8379). E6 is confounded three ways (smear, staircase, frequency resolution). I10 isolates the staircase.
